question: Will the Federal Reserve cut interest rates by 25bp or more at the June 17, 2026 FOMC meeting?
textDescription: The June 16-17, 2026 FOMC meeting is the next Fed decision after the April 28-29 hold. CME FedWatch as of May 4, 2026 shows 96-98% probability of HOLD; Polymarket shows 95.5% NO-cut. March CPI ran 3.26% YoY; Fed has been cautious despite 4 dissenting committee votes for cuts. RESOLVES YES if, at the June 17 FOMC announcement, the Federal Reserve reduces the federal funds target range by 25 basis points or more. Source: Federal Reserve press release. RESOLVES NO if rates are held or raised. A reduction by exactly 25bp counts as YES.
newsShortReason: Federal Reserve officials favor rate hikes if inflation stays high, with no cuts expected in June.
newsKeyEvidence: Minutes from the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting reveal a majority of officials believe rate hikes may be necessary if inflation remains persistently above the 2% target. ([axios.com](https://www.axios.com/2026/05/20/fed-rates-minutes-warsh?utm_source=openai)) | The Federal Reserve held the federal funds rate at 4.25%–4.50% on May 7, 2026, indicating a cautious approach to monetary policy. ([investozora.com](https://investozora.com/fed-rate-hold-may-7-2026-fomc-decision/?utm_source=openai)) | The next FOMC meeting is scheduled for June 16-17, 2026, with no indications of a rate cut at that time. ([federalreserve.gov](https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy.htm?utm_source=openai))
